content: We can reframe general autonomous agent design around the principle that *all* agent exploration trajectories, even fully failed ones that do not reach any extrinsic or intrinsic reward milestone, count as self-supervised training data to prune misaligned world model predictions and refine active memory retrieval for future exploration. This creates a 'failure-as-dataset' loop where agents do not discard failed trajectories, instead using them to continuously shrink the state space of unproductive exploration actions without requiring external labeled reward signals.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
trigger_topic: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
